topTargets.name: Carnell Tate
topTargets.reasoning: If Love goes 1.01 to FRA, Tate is the medium-affinity slam dunk at 1.02 — Consensus #2 Tier 1 (TEN), RSP #1 with the highest DoT score in the entire 2026 class (92.3, Franchise grade, Moss comp), and MFL ADP #2 rookie (9.46). All three boards agree, which is exactly what a 50/25/25 weighting rewards.

topTargets.name: Jeremiyah Love
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #1 Tier 1 (ARI), RSP #2 DoT 89.3 Starter grade with All-Pro upside (Kamara comp), ADP #2 rookie (3.59 — actually the cheapest of the elite tier). If FRA somehow passes at 1.01 (unlikely), Love is the no-brainer at 1.02 even with 8 RBs already rostered — talent this clean across all three boards trumps positional balance.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #3 Tier 1 (NO), RSP #10 DoT 84.5 Rotational Starter, ADP #18 (12.71). The third-best WR on Consensus and a clean fallback if both Love and Tate are gone. Medium-affinity GM doesn't reach past Consensus Tier 1 here — Tyson is the floor.

topTargets.name: Makai Lemon
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #4 Tier 1 (PHI), RSP #3 DoT 88.8 Starter grade (Amon-Ra comp), ADP #11 (11.18). Strong on all three boards. Pivot option if the top two go and the GM prefers PHI landing spot over Tyson's NO situation.

topTargets.name: Antonio Williams
topTargets.reasoning: At 3.14, this is the kind of value a 50/25/25 GM hunts: Consensus #24 Tier 4 (WAS) but RSP #18 DoT 83.9 Rotational Starter (Jayden Reed comp) and ADP #55 (29.06). RSP flag plus consensus presence makes him a defensible upside swing late in round 3.

capPosture: Healthy at $32.1M of $45M used ($12.9M space) with 4 expirings — can comfortably active-roster the 1.02 rookie at full salary; no need to manipulate taxi for cap relief.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: Roster has only 2 TEs — a real positional thin spot. Stowers is Consensus #21 Tier 3 (PHI), RSP #11 DoT 84.4 (classic move TE, on cusp of Starter tier), ADP #35 (21.25). At 3.14 he could still be on the board, and a medium-affinity GM trusts the RSP signal enough to grab a TE2 with starter ceiling over another WR dart.

summary: Dead Cap Walking is in a 'take the best player available' posture at 1.02 — cap is healthy enough to absorb the rookie active, and the medium-affinity 50/25/25 weighting points squarely at Tate (or Love if FRA passes), since both win on Consensus, RSP, and ADP simultaneously. The 3.14 pick is a developmental swing, with the TE-thin roster pushing Stowers as a credible target alongside RSP-loved WR fliers like Antonio Williams.
